bugs.title: FR50 --verbose flag is completely non-functional (Verbosef is dead code)
bugs.description: The --verbose / -v / STAGEHAND_VERBOSE flag (PRD FR50, a P1 requirement) produces ZERO observable output. The ui.Output.Verbosef method — documented as 'the FR50 sink for the resolved command, raw agent stdout, and retry traces' — is defined in internal/ui/output.go but NEVER CALLED anywhere in production code (only in its own unit tests). Furthermore, pkg/stagehand.GenerateCommit (the function the CLI's default action delegates to) constructs its own ui.Output with verbose hardcoded to false: `ui.NewOutput(os.Stdout, os.Stderr, false, false)`. Even if Verbosef were called, the generate step could never emit verbose output. The cmd/stagehand/run.go file explicitly acknowledges this gap in a comment ('Full FR50 verbose ... is not observable through the GENERATE step in v1') but ships it anyway. For a tool whose entire value proposition is shelling out to external agent CLIs, the inability to inspect the resolved command, raw agent stdout, and retry attempts makes provider debugging effectively impossible.
bugs.reproduction: 1. Create a repo with a staged change and a working stub agent provider (defined in .stagehand.toml). 2. Run `stagehand --provider <name> --verbose` (or `-v`, or STAGEHAND_VERBOSE=1). 3. Observe stderr: there is NO resolved provider command, NO raw agent stdout, NO retry-attempt tracing — stderr is empty on success. Verified empirically: `stagehand --provider myagent --verbose 2>/tmp/stderr.txt` produced an empty stderr file on a successful commit. A grep for `.Verbosef(` across internal/generate, internal/provider, cmd/stagehand, and pkg/stagehand (excluding _test.go) returns zero matches.
bugs.expected: Per FR50: '--verbose / -v / STAGEHAND_VERBOSE=1 — print the resolved provider command, the raw agent stdout, and each retry attempt to stderr.' When verbose is enabled, stderr should show the fully-rendered agent command (argv + stdin delivery), the raw unparsed stdout returned by the agent, and each parse/duplicate-retry attempt.
bugs.actual: The --verbose flag is parsed and stored in cfg.Verbose and passed to the CLI's ui.NewOutput, but (a) nothing in the generate/executor/provider packages ever calls Verbosef, and (b) GenerateCommit creates its own ui.Output with verbose=false, discarding the resolved cfg.Verbose entirely. stderr stays empty regardless of the flag.
bugs.location: internal/ui/output.go (Verbosef defined, never called); pkg/stagehand/stagehand.go:212 (`ui.NewOutput(os.Stdout, os.Stderr, false, false)`); cmd/stagehand/run.go:~250 (known-gap comment). Fixes require: (1) thread cfg.Verbose into GenerateCommit's Deps.Output (e.g. via Options or by having the CLI pass its already-configured *ui.Output), and (2) add Verbosef call sites in internal/provider/executor.go (resolved command + raw stdout) and internal/generate/generate.go (retry attempts).
bugs.suggestedFix: Add Verbosef calls: in executor.Run after rendering the command (log m.Command + r.Args + whether stdin is used), after capturing stdout (log the raw stdout); in generate.CommitStaged at each inner/outer loop iteration (log attempt number, parse result ok/not-ok, duplicate-rejection reason). Pass the verbose-enabled *ui.Output from the CLI into GenerateCommit instead of constructing a fresh verbose=false one — e.g. add a Verbose field to stagehand.Options or accept a pre-built ui.Output.

bugs.title: Non-sentinel errors produce a SILENT exit 1 with no diagnostic (FR8 merge-conflict, not-a-repo, git failures)
bugs.description: cmd/stagehand/run.go's runDefault passes errors from maybeAutoStage and non-sentinel errors from stagehand.GenerateCommit directly to mapErrorToExitCode(err) WITHOUT ever printing the error message. mapErrorToExitCode only translates an error into an exit-code int; it discards the message. As a result, any error that is not one of the four recognized sentinels (ErrNothingToCommit, ErrRescue, ErrHeadMoved, ErrNothingStaged) yields exit code 1 with NO output at all. This directly violates FR8 ('If write-tree fails (e.g., unresolved merge conflicts in the index), abort with a CLEAR error before any generation') and produces a confusing silent failure for several common scenarios. The git layer (internal/git) already produces clear, helpful messages (e.g. 'git: unresolved merge conflicts in index (resolve them before generating): ...'), but runDefault swallows them.
bugs.reproduction: MERGE CONFLICT: `git init; echo a>f; git add f; git commit -m init; git checkout -b b; echo b>f; git commit -am b2; git checkout master; echo m>f; git commit -am m2; git merge b` (creates a conflict), then with a file staged run `stagehand --provider <any>`. Result: exit 1, NO message (FR8 expects 'Resolve merge conflicts first'). NOT-A-REPO: `cd /tmp/empty && stagehand --provider <any>`. Result: exit 1, completely silent. Verified: both produced empty stderr/stdout with exit code 1.
bugs.expected: FR8: a clear error message before any generation when write-tree fails (merge conflicts). Generally (PRD §18.2 failure-mode table + good UX): every error path should print a human-readable explanation. E.g. 'Resolve merge conflicts first.' / 'not a git repository' / the wrapped git error text.
bugs.actual: Silent exit 1. The error message from the git layer (or from GenerateCommit's internal config.Load/git.New/provider-resolution) is passed to mapErrorToExitCode, which returns int(1) and the message is lost. The user sees a prompt return to shell with exit code 1 and zero output, with no clue what went wrong.
bugs.location: cmd/stagehand/run.go runDefault(): the two call sites `if err := maybeAutoStage(...); err != nil { return mapErrorToExitCode(err) }` and `_, err = stagehand.GenerateCommit(...); return mapErrorToExitCode(err)` — neither prints err. Contrast with the earlier error paths in the same function which DO print via out.Progressf.
bugs.suggestedFix: In runDefault, before returning mapErrorToExitCode(err), print any non-sentinel error to stderr via out.Progressf (the sentinel errors ErrRescue/ErrHeadMoved/ErrNothingToCommit already print their own messages inside CommitStaged/maybeAutoStage, so guard with errors.Is checks to avoid double-printing, or simply print errors that are not one of the four sentinels). Concretely: `if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, stagehand.ErrRescue) && !errors.Is(err, stagehand.ErrHeadMoved) && !errors.Is(err, stagehand.ErrNothingToCommit) && !errors.Is(err, ErrNothingStaged) { out.Progressf("stagehand: %s\n", err) }`.

bugs.title: `providers list` and `providers show` ignore --config and STAGEHAND_* env flags
bugs.description: The providers subcommands (FR46 list / FR47 show) call `config.Load(config.Flags{}, "")` with an EMPTY Flags struct, ignoring the global --config, --provider, --model flags and all STAGEHAND_* environment variables (FR34 layers 6-7). The PRD §15.2 defines --config as a GLOBAL flag ('Path to a config file (overrides discovery)'), so it must apply to subcommands. A user who defines a custom provider in an external config file and runs `stagehand --config myconfig.toml providers list` will NOT see their custom provider, and the resolved default will not reflect --provider/STAGEHAND_PROVIDER. Only repo-local .stagehand.toml discovery works (because readRepoFile uses the cwd). The code comment even admits 'the flags layer is empty because the persistent flags are not yet wired by P1.M7.T2.S1' — but P1.M7.T2 has since shipped the flag wiring for the default action, and these subcommands were never updated to use it.
bugs.reproduction: 1. Create /tmp/myconf.toml containing `[provider.customagent] command="/bin/echo" detect="echo" ...`. 2. Run `stagehand providers list --config /tmp/myconf.toml`. 3. Observe: 'customagent' does NOT appear (only the 6 built-ins). 4. From a directory whose repo-local .stagehand.toml defines the same provider, run `stagehand providers list` (no --config) — 'customagent' DOES appear. Also: `STAGEHAND_CONFIG=/tmp/myconf.toml stagehand providers list` likewise omits customagent. Confirmed empirically.
bugs.expected: Per FR34/§15.2: the --config flag (and STAGEHAND_* env) are global and apply to ALL commands. `stagehand providers list --config X` should load X's [provider.*] definitions and resolved default, and `--provider`/STAGEHAND_PROVIDER should influence the '(default)' marker.
bugs.actual: providers list/show pass config.Flags{} (no env, no flag layer) to config.Load, so --config, --provider, --model, and STAGEHAND_* are all ignored. The default shown always reflects only file/git-config discovery, and external-config providers are invisible.
bugs.location: cmd/stagehand/providers.go: newProvidersListCmd RunE `config.Load(config.Flags{}, "")` and newProvidersShowCmd RunE `config.Load(config.Flags{}, "")`. The default action (cmd/stagehand/run.go runDefault) correctly uses buildFlags(cmd) → config.Load(flags, "."), so the wiring helper already exists; it just needs to be reused here.
bugs.suggestedFix: Replace `config.Load(config.Flags{}, "")` in both subcommand RunE closures with the same buildFlags(cmd) → config.Load(flags, ".") pattern used by runDefault (the persistent flags are already registered on rootCmd and inherited by subcommands, so cmd.Flags().Changed works).

bugs.title: Generation timeout returns exit code 3 (rescue) instead of the PRD-mandated 124
bugs.description: PRD §15.4 exit-code table explicitly reserves 124 for 'Timeout (generation exceeded --timeout)'. The implementation instead collapses a generation timeout into the rescue path and returns exit code 3 (ErrRescue). mapErrorToExitCode's comment acknowledges this: 'ui.ExitTimeout (124) is therefore RESERVED for a future CLI-level deadline and is not returned in v1.' A script or wrapper (e.g. a lazygit customCommand or CI step) that branches on exit code 124 to detect timeouts will not fire. The underlying behavior is SAFE (the agent is killed and the rescue block prints the tree SHA + manual recovery), so this is purely a spec-compliance/exit-code deviation, not a data-integrity issue.
bugs.reproduction: 1. Configure a hanging agent provider (e.g. a script that sleeps forever). 2. Stage a change. 3. Run `stagehand --provider hangagent --timeout 3s`. 4. Observe exit code: 3 (not 124). Verified: `time stagehand --provider hangagent --timeout 3s` killed the agent at ~3.0s and returned exit 3 with the rescue block.
bugs.expected: PRD §15.4: exit code 124 when generation exceeds --timeout. (The §18.2 failure table writes '124/3', which is ambiguous, but §15.4 is the authoritative exit-code reference and lists 124.)
bugs.actual: Exit code 3 (ErrRescue) for a generation timeout. The rescue block is printed correctly and the repo is left unchanged; only the exit code differs from the spec.
bugs.location: cmd/stagehand/run.go mapErrorToExitCode() — a timeout returned by executor.Run (*provider.TimeoutError, wrapping context.DeadlineExceeded) propagates through generate.CommitStaged as ErrRescue, which maps to ui.ExitRescue (3). There is no branch detecting the timeout type to return ui.ExitTimeout (124).
bugs.suggestedFix: In CommitStaged or mapErrorToExitCode, distinguish a timeout (*provider.TimeoutError / errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded)) from other rescue triggers and return/map to ui.ExitTimeout (124). The rescue block should still print (the snapshot exists), so the fix is only in exit-code selection. Alternatively, document explicitly in the README that timeouts surface as exit 3; but matching the §15.4 table is preferable.

bugs.title: Auto-stage notice uses non-pluralized '(N files)' for N==1 (e.g. '1 files')
bugs.description: The FR18 auto-stage notice 'Nothing staged — staging all changes (N files).' uses a hardcoded 'files' plural with no singular form, producing the grammatically incorrect 'staging all changes (1 files).' when exactly one file is staged. Verified empirically: auto-staging a single untracked file printed 'Nothing staged — staging all changes (1 files).'
bugs.reproduction: 1. In a clean repo, create exactly one untracked file. 2. Run `stagehand --provider <any>`. 3. Observe the notice reads 'Nothing staged — staging all changes (1 files).' instead of '(1 file)'.
bugs.expected: Correct English pluralization: '1 file', 'N files' for N != 1.
bugs.actual: 'staging all changes (1 files).' regardless of count.
bugs.location: cmd/stagehand/stage.go maybeAutoStage: `out.Progressf("Nothing staged — staging all changes (%d files).\n", n)`.
bugs.suggestedFix: Trivial: `files := "files"; if n == 1 { files = "file" }` then use (%d %s), or pre-format the noun.

summary: End-to-end creative QA was performed against the PRD: happy-path commit, stage-while-generating (the §13.4 core differentiator), snapshot/CAS safety (HEAD-moved concurrency), SIGINT rescue, generation timeout, duplicate-rejection retry loop, dry-run, auto-stage-all, --all flag, config precedence, providers list/show, config init/path, and the public library API (pkg/stagehand.GenerateCommit). The headline snapshot-based atomic-commit machinery is SOLID and faithful to the PRD: stage-while-generating correctly keeps later-staged files out of the in-flight commit and leaves them staged; the CAS update-ref refuses a moved HEAD and prints the recovery command; SIGINT/timeout trigger the rescue block and leave the repo byte-for-byte unchanged; duplicate subjects are detected and retried. The build is clean and all unit/integration tests pass. Five defects were found, all outside the core safety machinery: (1) FR50 --verbose is entirely non-functional (Verbosef is dead code and GenerateCommit hardcodes verbose=false); (2) non-sentinel git errors (merge conflicts per FR8, not-a-repo, git failures) produce a SILENT exit 1 because runDefault discards the message via mapErrorToExitCode; (3) providers list/show ignore the global --config and STAGEHAND_* flags; (4) generation timeout returns exit 3 instead of the PRD §15.4 exit 124; (5) a grammar nit ('1 files'). No critical (data-loss/security/core-blocker) issues were found.
recommendations: HIGHEST PRIORITY: Fix BUG-002 (silent failures) — it directly violates FR8 (a P0 requirement) and breaks basic UX for common scenarios like merge conflicts and running outside a repo. The fix is a few lines in runDefault (print non-sentinel errors before mapping to exit code).

HIGH PRIORITY: Fix BUG-001 (non-functional --verbose) — FR50 is a P1 requirement and verbose tracing is essential for users debugging provider/agent invocation issues. Requires both threading cfg.Verbose into GenerateCommit's Output and adding Verbosef call sites in the executor and generate loop.
MEDIUM PRIORITY: Fix BUG-003 (providers subcommands ignore flags) — reuse the existing buildFlags(cmd) helper in providers.go so --config/STAGEHAND_* are honored consistently across all commands.
LOW PRIORITY: Resolve BUG-004 (timeout exit code 124 vs 3) to match the §15.4 exit-code table; the behavior is already safe.
LOW PRIORITY: Fix BUG-005 (grammar) — trivial pluralization in the FR18 notice.
The snapshot/CAS/signal-handling/rescue/dedupe core needs no changes — its invariants held under adversarial concurrency, signal, timeout, and duplicate tests.
